steps: Go to https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps and log in with your Dropbox account
Select 'Scoped access' as the API type
Choose access type: 'App folder' (limited to one folder) or 'Full Dropbox' (access all files)
Enter a unique name for your app and click 'Create app'
On the app settings page, note the 'App key' (this is your client_id)
Click 'Show' next to 'App secret' to reveal your client_secret — copy both values
Under 'OAuth 2', add your redirect URI(s) and enable the required permission scopes under 'Permissions'

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: App Key (Client ID)
credential_fields.description: Your Dropbox app's App Key, shown on the app settings page in the App Console.

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: App Secret (Client Secret)
credential_fields.description: Your Dropbox app's App Secret, shown on the app settings page.

notes: Free Dropbox accounts work for development and testing
App Folder access is recommended for apps that only need their own storage space
Required scopes must be enabled under the 'Permissions' tab before generating tokens
You can generate a short-lived access token directly from the app console for quick testing
Refresh tokens are long-lived; access tokens expire after 4 hours by default
